description_long: This sculpture depicts a weathered, partially broken classical figure seated atop a fluted column pedestal. The figure is missing its head and lower legs, with only the torso, one arm, and part of the other arm remaining. The column is cylindrical with vertical grooves and sits on a square base, both showing signs of moss, dirt, and erosion, suggesting age and exposure to the elements. The surface is rough and uneven, with visible chips and cracks, especially around the broken edges of the figure. The coloration is predominantly a mix of gray stone and greenish moss, giving it an ancient, abandoned appearance. The overall form evokes the ruins of Greco-Roman statuary commonly found in parks or historical urban spaces.
description: A weathered, broken classical figure sits atop a mossy, fluted column pedestal, with rough stone and greenish hues. The sculpture is missing its head and lower limbs, evoking the look of ancient ruins.
description_view_0: The front view shows the broken torso and arm of the figure seated on the fluted column, with the most detail visible on the chest and upper arm.
description_view_1: The rear view reveals the back of the torso and the column, with less detail on the figure and more focus on the column's grooves and mossy base.
description_view_2: This angled view highlights the side of the figure, showing the broken edge of the arm and the curvature of the column.
description_view_3: The opposite side view displays the remaining arm draped over the column, with significant moss and erosion visible on the surface.
attribute_subject_matter: fragmented classical human figure seated on a fluted column
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible, but the moss and weathering suggest an outdoor environment such as a park or garden.
